learning_outcomes.description: Define the formal environmental impacts assessment(EIA)methodology and overall approach.
Describe the overall interactions and links between the EIA process and domains such as planning and management as part of a sound decision making process for choices in environmental management.
Understand the critical aspects of different tools and instruments within the EIA approach that make them more or less suited to use by practitioners including policymakers, planners and managers.
Understand the biophysical and social dimensions of specific environmental problems as they relate to management outcomes and the approaches taken to address them.
Apply their understanding of the role of science and other types of knowledge in environmental policy, planning and management to decision making in assessment and monitoring.
Apply knowledge of the EIA process in the development of an appropriate monitoring & assessment program.

assessments.task_description:
Students will need to answer all 15 multiple choice true/false questions (1% per correct answer out of 15%) that will be made available on Blackboard. 

 This is an Open Book Quiz

This quiz will cover lectures 1 to 6 and will consist of multiple choice, true/false questions taken directly from the lectures and reading set for students in both the lectures and contact sessions.

The quiz opens from 8am on Monday the 7th September and closes at 2pm on Friday the 11th September. There is a 5-day window to take the 15-minute quiz. 

You are allowed one (1) attempt within the 5-day window. Please make sure you answer all the questions. 

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The quiz is to be taken online and submitted through Blackboard by the due date.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: Exams submitted after the end of the submission time will incur a late penalty.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Students need to select any EIA of their own choice and using their knowledge of the processes involved in EIA's gained through the lectures write a report on the EIA they have chosen. The student must discuss whether or not their chosen EIA meets present requirements of an EIA, justifying and identify why it does or does not meet these requirements. 

The report must have 5 scientific references in the report, with referencing following the Australian Harvard referencing style.

The word limit for the Report is 4500 words +/- 10%.

It is expected that ENVM7206 students will achieve a higher standard of research and report writing skills. Please read the marking criteria on Blackboard in this regard.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Online submission by Turnitin only by the due date and time. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets are required. Submission via email is not accepted.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
End of Semester Exam Period

7/11/2026 - 21/11/2026

This exam requires attendance on campus, or at an approved off-campus exam centre.
assessments.task_description:
• This exam consists of 12 short‑answer questions, each linked to a lecture topic.
  • You must answer any 6 questions of your choice.
  • Each question is worth 5% of the course grade (total = 30%).
  • Recommended length: 150–300 words per answer.
  • Write clearly and concisely. Support your answers with examples where appropriate.
  • Time allowed: 120 minutes – 20 minutes per question
  • Materials permitted: None.

This exam requires attendance on campus, or at an approved off-campus exam centre. You must be able to attend one of these venues. Please refer to the Off-Campus exams information page for further information. Please note a digital exam is not available for this assessment.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 1 - Introduction to the EIA Process

This lecture defines the EIA process and relates it to the overall adaptive approach to environmental management.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 1 - Introduction to the EIA Process This lecture defines the EIA process and relates it to the overall adaptive approach to environmental management.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 2 - AEM, Regulatory Frameworks.

This lecture explores AEM and the regulatory, policy, and governance motivations and drivers behind the EIA process at a local, national and international level.
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 2 - AEM, Regulatory Frameworks. This lecture explores AEM and the regulatory, policy, and governance motivations and drivers behind the EIA process at a local, national and international level.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 3 - The EIA Process in Detail.

This lecture takes students through the environmental impact assessment process in detail including its link to the development of ensuing environmental monitoring and assessment programs.
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 3 - The EIA Process in Detail. This lecture takes students through the environmental impact assessment process in detail including its link to the development of ensuing environmental monitoring and assessment programs.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 4 - Social-Cultural Impact Assessment

As an integral part of EIA, this lecture covers social & cultural impact assessment methods and encompasses indigenous issues as a specific aspect of this.
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 4 - Social-Cultural Impact Assessment As an integral part of EIA, this lecture covers social & cultural impact assessment methods and encompasses indigenous issues as a specific aspect of this.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 5 - Socio-Economic Impact Assessment

This lecture presents & discusses tools for economic assessment from both an application perspective as well as how they may be used to support planning & management beyond the EIA.
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 5 - Socio-Economic Impact Assessment This lecture presents & discusses tools for economic assessment from both an application perspective as well as how they may be used to support planning & management beyond the EIA.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 6 - Inside the EIA: Risk Assessment

In contrast to some of the other methods this component examines how risk assessment can be a core element of the EIA process and provide key outputs for any ensuing monitoring or assessment.
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 6 - Inside the EIA: Risk Assessment In contrast to some of the other methods this component examines how risk assessment can be a core element of the EIA process and provide key outputs for any ensuing monitoring or assessment.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 7 - Adaptive Environmental Management (AEM).

This lecture looks at Adaptive Environmental Management
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 7 - Adaptive Environmental Management (AEM). This lecture looks at Adaptive Environmental Management

learning_activities.topic:
Week 8 - Statistics, QA and QC in the EIA.

This lecture gives an overview of current and emerging methods in statistics that underpin assessment & monitoring design within current EIA practise.
learning_activities.topic_title: Week 8 - Statistics, QA and QC in the EIA.
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 8 - Statistics, QA and QC in the EIA. This lecture gives an overview of current and emerging methods in statistics that underpin assessment & monitoring design within current EIA practise.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 9 - Using a Systems Approach & Modelling

This lecture explores the fundamentals of systems approaches to environmental impact assessment and a range of modelling tools useful within the EIA process and its design.
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 9 - Using a Systems Approach & Modelling This lecture explores the fundamentals of systems approaches to environmental impact assessment and a range of modelling tools useful within the EIA process and its design.
In-Semester Break (No Classes) No lectures or practicals

learning_activities.topic:
Week 10 - Kings Birthday public holiday

No lecture due to Kings Birthday public holiday
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 10 - Kings Birthday public holiday No lecture due to Kings Birthday public holiday

learning_activities.topic:
Week 11 - Case Study & Monitoring

This lecture examines a case study and monitoring element of the EIA process which is critical in the design and reporting of the EIA outcomes.
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 11 - Case Study & Monitoring This lecture examines a case study and monitoring element of the EIA process which is critical in the design and reporting of the EIA outcomes.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 12 - Maximising its value.

This lecture uses current case studies to demonstrate how to maximise the value and ultimate usefulness of an EIA as applied to different problem sets.
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 12 - Maximising its value. This lecture uses current case studies to demonstrate how to maximise the value and ultimate usefulness of an EIA as applied to different problem sets.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 13 - Application of ESIA in International Dev

This lecture looks at the application of ESIA in International Development Projects
learning_activities.topic_title: Week 13 - Application of ESIA in International Dev
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 13 - Application of ESIA in International Dev This lecture looks at the application of ESIA in International Development Projects
